items.title: Build login interface and client-side validation
items.description: Deliver a user-friendly login screen with required fields, masked password entry, submission controls, and clear validation messaging aligned with the SRS.
acceptance_criteria: Login screen includes email/username input, password input, login button, app logo/title, and a visible validation/error area.
Password input masks characters during entry and never renders plain text in the UI.
Empty required fields are blocked before authentication submission and show clear validation feedback.
When email format validation is applicable, invalid email format is detected and a user-facing error is shown.
Layout is responsive and usable on modern desktop and mobile browsers.

items.title: As a registered user, I can enter credentials on a login form
items.description: Provide the login form experience so users can submit username/email and password from a clear, accessible interface.
acceptance_criteria: User can open a dedicated login page when unauthenticated.
Form provides username/email and password inputs and a Login button.
Password field masks entered characters.
The login page renders consistently in supported modern browsers.

items.title: Implement login page UI components
items.description: Create the login page with app title/logo, credential fields, login button, and validation message container.
acceptance_criteria: Login page contains app title/logo, username/email field, password field, login button, and message area.
All fields and controls are keyboard accessible with visible focus states.
UI matches approved style guidelines for spacing, labels, and control states.

items.title: Add client-side required-field and email-format validation
items.description: Validate empty username/email and password fields before submit and apply email format checks when email identifiers are used.
acceptance_criteria: Submitting with empty username/email shows an explicit field error.
Submitting with empty password shows an explicit field error.
Invalid email format is rejected with a clear message when an email value is entered.
Valid inputs proceed to server authentication request without client-side validation errors.

items.title: Implement user-friendly login error presentation
items.description: Display clear, understandable validation and authentication errors without exposing sensitive credential details.
acceptance_criteria: Validation errors appear near the related field or at form level per UI requirement.
Authentication failures display only a generic message such as invalid username/email or password.
Error states clear appropriately when the user corrects input and resubmits.

items.title: Implement authentication, session lifecycle, and logout
items.description: Provide secure credential verification, session creation/maintenance, successful-login redirect behavior, and logout session termination.
acceptance_criteria: Submitted credentials are authenticated against stored user records.
Valid credentials create an authenticated session and route user to dashboard/home.
Invalid credentials are denied without disclosing whether username/email or password failed.
Authenticated session persists until logout or configured session expiry.
Logout terminates the active session and redirects to login page.

items.title: As a registered user, I can log in and reach my authorized landing page
items.description: Authenticate valid credentials and redirect authenticated users to the dashboard/home page.
acceptance_criteria: System verifies submitted credentials against persisted user records.
Valid credentials return a success result and establish an authenticated session/token.
After successful authentication, user is redirected to dashboard/home.
Invalid credentials are rejected and user remains on login page with a generic error.

items.title: Build backend credential authentication endpoint
items.description: Implement server-side authentication logic to compare submitted credentials with stored account data.
acceptance_criteria: Endpoint accepts username/email and password payload over HTTPS.
Credential verification uses hashed password comparison against stored user record.
Valid credentials produce authenticated success response with session/token payload.
Invalid credentials produce a failure response mapped to generic login error behavior.

items.title: Implement post-login redirect flow
items.description: Ensure authenticated users are routed from login to the authorized landing page after successful authentication.
acceptance_criteria: On successful authentication, client navigates user to dashboard/home route.
If redirect target is unavailable, system falls back to a safe default authorized page.
Redirect logic runs only after authenticated state/session is established.

items.title: As an authenticated user, I can log out securely
items.description: Provide logout capability that terminates active session and returns user to login page.
acceptance_criteria: Authenticated UI exposes a logout action.
Logout invalidates the active session/token server-side.
After logout, user is redirected to login page.
After logout, previously protected pages are no longer accessible without re-authentication.

items.title: Add logout control and handler
items.description: Expose logout in authenticated navigation and invoke session termination workflow.
acceptance_criteria: Logout control is visible only when user is authenticated.
Triggering logout calls backend/session termination endpoint.
Client clears local authentication state and navigates to login page on success.

items.title: Implement server-side session invalidation on logout
items.description: Terminate active session/token artifacts when logout is requested.
acceptance_criteria: Logout request marks active session/token as invalid for subsequent requests.
System returns success response when session invalidation completes.
Subsequent requests with invalidated session/token are treated as unauthenticated.

items.title: Enforce access control and security hardening for login flow
items.description: Protect restricted routes, enforce secure transport and credential handling, and apply session security controls for unauthenticated access attempts.
acceptance_criteria: Unauthenticated users are prevented from accessing protected routes and redirected to login.
Authentication failures never disclose whether username/email or password is incorrect.
Credentials and authentication traffic are handled only via secure protocols.
Session inactivity timeout is defined and enforced.
Password storage uses hashed format and plain-text display is prevented.

items.title: As an unauthenticated user, I am redirected away from protected pages
items.description: Ensure protected routes require authentication and redirect unauthorized access attempts to login.
acceptance_criteria: Protected routes check authenticated state before rendering restricted content.
Unauthenticated access attempt redirects to login page.
After successful login, user can access previously requested protected area if supported by routing design.

items.title: Implement route guard middleware for protected pages
items.description: Create reusable middleware/guard checks that block unauthenticated requests and issue login redirects.
acceptance_criteria: Each protected route invokes shared authentication guard logic.
Guard denies unauthenticated requests and triggers redirect to login endpoint/page.
Authenticated requests pass through and render requested protected resource.

items.title: As a security stakeholder, I need hardened authentication controls
items.description: Implement key security NFRs for password protection, secure transport, and non-revealing authentication failure handling.
acceptance_criteria: System uses HTTPS for login and authentication-related requests in deployed environments.
Password values are stored and verified as hashed values, not plaintext.
Authentication failure message remains generic and does not reveal credential component validity.
Sensitive authentication logs do not include plaintext passwords.

items.title: Enforce HTTPS and secure authentication transport settings
items.description: Configure application and infrastructure integration points to require secure transport for login flows.
acceptance_criteria: Authentication endpoints reject or redirect insecure HTTP requests according to deployment policy.
Application configuration documents required TLS/HTTPS settings for environments.
Security verification confirms no credential submission occurs over plaintext transport.

items.title: Implement hashed password storage and verification
items.description: Ensure credentials are stored and checked using approved password hashing algorithms.
acceptance_criteria: User password records are stored as salted hash values.
Authentication compares submitted password to stored hash with approved verifier.
No code path logs or returns plaintext password data.

items.title: As a product owner, I need clearly defined session timeout behavior
items.description: Define and implement inactivity-based session expiry to satisfy security and usability requirements.
acceptance_criteria: A specific inactivity timeout duration is documented and approved.
Session automatically expires after configured inactivity period.
Expired session forces re-authentication on next protected action.
Timeout behavior is covered by automated tests.

items.title: Implement inactivity-based session expiration
items.description: Add session expiry checks that invalidate sessions after agreed inactivity duration.
acceptance_criteria: Session inactivity timestamp updates on authenticated activity.
Sessions exceeding configured inactivity duration are invalidated.
Expired sessions cannot access protected endpoints and are redirected to login.
